classes.name: Water
classes.description: Open water body — remaining Aral Sea surface. High NDWI (> 0.05). Appears dark blue/black in NIR view.

classes.name: Land
classes.description: Dry land, exposed lakebed (salt flats / Aralkum Desert), or vegetated areas. Low or negative NDWI. Appears bright in SWIR view.

True Colour (RGB).description: Natural colour composite. B04=Red, B03=Green, B02=Blue. Use for general orientation.

False Colour (NIR-R-G).description: B08=Red, B04=Green, B03=Blue. Water appears dark/black. Vegetation appears bright red. Best view for water/land boundary.

NDWI.description: Normalised Difference Water Index = (B03 - B08) / (B03 + B08). Positive values (bright) = water. Negative values (dark) = land. Primary discriminator for this project.

SWIR (B11).description: Shortwave Infrared band. Salt flats and bare soil appear very bright. Water appears very dark. Useful for distinguishing dry lakebed from vegetated land.

SWIR Composite (SWIR-NIR-G).description: B11=Red, B08=Green, B03=Blue. Salt flats appear magenta/pink. Water is black. Vegetation is green. Ideal for distinguishing salt flat from open land.

NIR (B08).description: Near-infrared band alone. Water absorbs NIR strongly — appears near-black. Land and salt flats reflect NIR — appear bright.

NIR Edges.description: Edge-detected NIR band. Highlights water/land boundaries precisely. Use to check where you should place your class boundary.
